text:
There once was a shy girl named Sarah who lived in a small town. She had curly red hair and glasses that sat perched on the bridge of her nose, always looking up at the world instead of down at her own shoes.

She loved stories. Her favorite books were fantasy novels, filled with mystical creatures and adventurous quests. One day, she stumbled upon a battered copy of "The Three Christs of York," which intrigued her greatly.

Every night, Sarah tucked her books into a pillow lined with velvet and fell asleep dreaming about dragons and angels.

One morning, she awoke to find a peculiar creature standing at her doorstep. It was a human with wings, scales, antlers on its head, and eyes like glowing gems. His name was Carl.

Carl was no ordinary visitor. He spoke fluently in dreams, predicting Sarah's desires before she could herself. Together, they explored dream worlds and shared their fantasies with gusto.

As the seasons changed, so did Sarah's relationships with her
